title: jit init default ruleset scaffolding and existing-repo migration
description:
Scaffold a default `.jit/rules.toml` on `jit init` and migrate existing repos' legacy config into rules, removing old keys without hard-erroring.

## Background

Since nothing is hard-coded, repos obtain their checks from `rules.toml`: new repos via `init`, existing repos via a one-time migration. A git-synced stale config carrying removed keys must not break. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 9) and decision record sections 8.2 and 8.4.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `jit init` scaffolds a default `.jit/rules.toml` reproducing the default checks.
- [ ] [hard] A one-time migration converts existing `[validation]` and `[namespaces].{values,pattern,required}` config into `rules.toml`, then removes the old enforcement keys.
- [ ] [hard] When a config still carrying removed keys is loaded before migration has run, the loader warns and ignores the unknown keys and prompts to migrate; it does not hard-error.
- [ ] [hard] Existing repos obtain defaults without silently losing validation.

## Notes

Anchors: init path at `main.rs:272` and `executor.init` in `commands/mod.rs`; config load in `config_manager.rs`.
